content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

Consider the following steps:


Choose an integer kkk (1≤k≤N)(1 \leq k \leq N)(1≤k≤N);
Divide the range [1,N][1, N][1,N] into kkk intervals [li,ri][l_i,r_i][li​,ri​] (1≤i≤k)(1 \leq i \le k)(1≤i≤k) such that:

l1=1l_1=1l1​=1 and rk=Nr_k=Nrk​=N;
li+1=ri+1l_{i+1}=r_i+1li+1​=ri​+1 for all (1≤i<k)(1 \leq i \lt k)(1≤i<k).


The score of this split is defined as k−∑i=1kmax⁡(Ali,Ali+1,…,Ari)k-\sum_{i=1}^k \max(A_{l_i},A_{l_{i}+1},\ldots,A_{r_i})k−∑i=1k​max(Ali​​,Ali​+1​,…,Ari​​).
Find the **maximum** possible score of a split across all 1≤k≤N1 \leq k \leq N1≤k≤N.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** possible score of a split across all 1≤k≤N1 \leq k \leq N1≤k≤N.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 4⋅1054\cdot 10^54⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose k=3k=3k=3 and [l1,r1]=[1,1],[l2,r2]=[2,2],[l3,r3]=[3,3][l_1,r_1]=[1,1],[l_2,r_2]=[2,2],[l_3,r_3]=[3,3][l1​,r1​]=[1,1],[l2​,r2​]=[2,2],[l3​,r3​]=[3,3].
The score of this split is k−(max⁡(A1)+max⁡(A2)+max⁡(A3))=3−(2−2+2)=1k-(\max(A_1)+\max(A_2)+\max(A_3))=3-(2-2+2)=1k−(max(A1​)+max(A2​)+max(A3​))=3−(2−2+2)=1. We can show that no other split for any kkk yields a score greater than −4-4−4.
Test case 222: Choose k=1k=1k=1 and [l1,r1]=[1,3][l_1,r_1]=[1,3][l1​,r1​]=[1,3].
The score of this split is k−(max⁡(A1,A2,A3))=1−5=−4k-(\max(A_1,A_2,A_3))=1-5=-4k−(max(A1​,A2​,A3​))=1−5=−4. We can show that no other split for any kkk yields a score greater than 111.
